clusters.title: Young mum accused of killing son in Gold Coast crash to stay in custody
clusters.neutral_headline: Young mum accused of killing son in Gold Coast crash to stay in custody

items.title: Young mum accused of killing son in Gold Coast crash to stay in custody
items.standfirst: Aneshia Daisy Case, 23, did not appear in court, four months after her three-year-old son Caden died at a police station.
items.content: A young mother accused of killing her child in a car crash will remain behind bars after her lawyer did not apply for bail.Aneshia Daisy Case, 23, did not appear in Brisbane Magistrates Court when her matter was mentioned today, four months after her three-year-old son Caden died at a police station.Case and the boy's father Samuel Ian Paterson, 24, have both been charged with manslaughter and drug trafficking following Caden's death.LIVE UPDATES: Trump claims 'entire' Iranian military leadership killed in strikesTheir son was critically injured after the family's white Mazda veered off the road and crashed in bushland north of the Gold Coast on October 25.A witness saw the car swerve off the road, before rushing the mother and Caden to Beenleigh Police Station.Officers at the station attempted CPR but the boy who suffered critical head injuries couldn't be saved, police said.WORLD: Iranian airstrikes may have cracked Dubai's safe, shiny façade foreverPaterson is accused of fleeing the accident and later being found at a nearby address with a one-year-old boy who was uninjured in the crash.Police will allege Case was behind the wheel at the time of the accident but have refused to comment on the fatal crash's cause, saying the investigation was "extremely complex".Case was not required to appear when her matter was mentioned today.Magistrate Julian Noud was told by Case's solicitor Skye O'Dwyer that there would be no bail application.Case was also charged with driving without a licence, possession of anything used in a crime and drug trafficking.Some of Case's charges arose from the Beenleigh jurisdiction and others from Southport, Ms O'Dwyer said."It may be prudent to split them at this early stage," she said, with the police prosecutor consenting.READ MORE: The men in line to take over from Iran's slain supreme leaderPolice earlier alleged the mother and children had travelled from the Gold Coast to collect the father from a Beenleigh address before the crash.Detective Acting Inspector Kent Ellis earlier told reporters the investigation had involved a forensic reconstruction of the crash and analysis of physical evidence along with CCTV footage."Determining who was driving at the time of impact required an extensive amount of investigative work," he said.Mr Noud ordered Case's manslaughter and unlicensed driving charges be next mentioned at Beenleigh on March 9.Case was remanded in custody and excused from appearing in person on her next court date.Her drug trafficking and another charge were moved to Southport.Paterson has been in custody since the crash after facing separate charges.He was remanded in custody to seek legal representation ahead of his next court appearance on March 30.CRIME: Man killed in alleged assault at NSW campgroundThe father was separately charged with domestic violence offences in October, when his emotional family claimed he would never harm his children. "We are sick of the f***ing lies," the boy's grandfather yelled at the media after the father's appearance at Southport Magistrates Court in October.READ MORE: How Middle East conflict could impact what Australians pay at the bowserNEVER MISS A STORY: Get your breaking news and exclusive stories first by following us across all platforms.Download the 9NEWS App here via Apple and Google PlayMake 9News your preferred source on Google by ticking this box hereSign up to our breaking newsletter here

items.title: Australia’s timid response to the US and Israel’s attack on Iran risks being seen as complicity | Donald Rothwell
items.standfirst: It is up to countries like Australia to stand up against aggression and make its position clear in support of international lawFollow our Australia news live blog for latest updatesGet our breaking news email, free app or daily news podcastInternational law matters when it comes to something as significant as a widescale military assault on a territory of another state, resulting in the death of its political and religious leader.The 1945 United Nations charter was written at the end of the second world war to place significant constraints around precisely that type of conduct, and yet 80 years later there is a clear sense that we are living in a world in which “might is right”. clusters.title: Man arrested after argument allegedly turns deadly at remote NSW campground
clusters.neutral_headline: Man arrested after argument allegedly turns deadly at remote NSW campground

items.title: Man arrested after argument allegedly turns deadly at remote NSW campground
items.standfirst: T he man arrested was camping with the 33-year-old man who died.
items.content: Police have arrested a man over the death of another man allegedly killed in an assault at a campground in northern New South Wales.Two men who were camping together with a woman are believed to have become involved in an argument at a campground off Point Lookout Road in Ebor, about 80 kilometres from Armidale.The altercation between the two men escalated when one seriously assaulted the other, leaving him with serious head injuries.LIVE UPDATES: Trump says Iran operation 'moving rapidly', three US troops killed WATCH: Missile fires behind Today reporter readying to go on airIt is not known how the head injuries were caused, nor if a weapon was used. The woman who was camping with the pair drove the injured man to Thora, calling friends who then alerted emergency services.However, by the time they reached the car, the man had died. "It's always a terrible thing that someone ends up dead from an altercation like this," Superintendent Chris McKinnon said."[He had] significant head injuries." READ MORE: Travel disruption continues as flights from Australia to Middle East scrapped Police were initially unable to find the other man involved, but at around 11.30am this morning they arrested a 26-year-old man on Waterfall Way in Ebor.It is believed he had received a lift to Ebor, but it is not known who drove him there.The man is known to police, McKinnon confirmed. He has been taken to Armidale Police Station, but charges have not been laid. McKinnon confirmed police were cur rently speaking with the woman who witnessed the alleged assault.NEVER MISS A STORY: Get your breaking news and exclusive stories first by following us across all platforms.Download the 9NEWS App here via Apple and Google PlayMake 9News your preferred source on Google by ticking this box hereSign up to our breaking newsletter here

items.title: Crocodile spotted in creek in Newcastle, Australia captured by wildlife experts – video
items.standfirst: An Australian freshwater crocodile has been captured in a Newcastle creek thousands of kilometres south of its normal habitat. Local residents were shocked to see the small reptile swimming in a small creek in a suburban park behind a Bunnings. The sighting occurred at Federal Park in Wallsend, close to a local pool and a primary school. A team of crocodile handlers, led by Billy Collett of the Australian Reptile Park, captured the freshwater crocodile on Sunday night after multiple attempts over the weekend Continue reading...

clusters.title: Baby left at fire station 'thriving' in foster care as authorities seek crucial info
clusters.neutral_headline: Newborn left at fire station 'thriving' amidst appeal for more information
bullet_summary: Even small details can make a profound difference
The baby boy found at a south Canberra fire station in November last year is "safe" and "thriving" in foster care, as the ACT government issues a fresh appeal for information

items.title: Baby left at fire station 'thriving' in foster care as authorities seek crucial info
items.standfirst: "This information will shape his life story. One day, he will want to understand where he came from. Even small details can make a profound difference."
items.content: A newborn baby abandoned at a fire station late last year is now thriving in foster care, as authorities seek crucial information about where he came from.The boy was just hours old when he was dropped off at South Tuggeranong Fire and Rescue Station in Conder, on the outskirts of Canberra, about 7.20am on November 27.He had been fed, was in good health and wrapped in blankets.READ MORE: One negative scan could have doomed Rachel to decades of agonyThe boy was immediately placed under the care of the territory's Children, Youth and Families department.ACT Health and Community Services Directorate deputy director-general Anne-Marie Sabellico said the boy was "thriving" with his foster family."He is safe, healthy, and his parents are meeting his every need," she said. However, decisions need to be made about the boy's care and long-term future."To do this well, we need information that only his family or those close to the situation get to know," Sabellico said."We're seeking details about any family, medical history, hospital or identity connections and any hopes his family has for him."This information will shape his life story. One day, he will want to understand where he came from. Even small details can make a profound difference." READ MORE: Potential oil price hike could impact petrol cost in AustraliaThe territory government has shared an image of the blanket the boy was wrapped in to appeal to his family, extended family or anyone close to the situation to come forward.It is a circle blanket depicting a doughnut with sprinkles.Sabellico stressed that the appeal was not about punishment or blame, but rather helping the boy have a better future.Information can be provided anonymously, and there is no expectation on anyone to be part of the boy's life going forward. "Without judgment, there's a reason why the little boy was left the way he was, so we want to respect that, but also understand it, to be able to move forward for this little boy," Sabellico said. Anyone with information can leave anonymous tips on Crime Stoppers.NEVER MISS A STORY: Get your breaking news and exclusive stories first by following us across all platforms.Download the 9NEWS App here via Apple and Google PlayMake 9News your preferred source on Google by ticking this box hereSign up to our breaking newsletter here

items.title: State TV presenter cries while announcing the death of Iran's supreme leader
items.standfirst: An Iranian state media presenter broke down in tears as he announced the death of Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei.
items.content: An Iranian state media presenter broke down in tears as he announced the death of Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei.The presenter told viewers the supreme leader, who was a key target in the US-Israel attacks, had "tasted the drink of martyrdom and joined the sublime heavenly kingdom of God" in a translated version of the broadcast.Others could be heard crying off-screen as the newsreader announced the news to the nation. LIVE UPDATES: New air attack warning sounds as fresh explosions heard READ MORE: Dubai Airport damaged in reported strike, four hurt Following the attack, President Donald Trump announced the leader had been killed.Iranian state media also confirmed that Khamenei is dead with a brief statement which said the supreme leader "has reached martyrdom".Khamenei, who has been supreme leader since 1989, was reportedly in his compound in Tehran when the joint assault was launched on military and government sites in Iran.Satellite images showed black smoke billowing from the compound, which appeared completely destroyed.NEVER MISS A STORY: Get your breaking news and exclusive stories first by following us across all platforms.Download the 9NEWS App here via Apple and Google PlayMake 9News your preferred source on Google by ticking this box hereSign up to our breaking newsletter here
